lightBloom.note: Warp core pulse is THE hero light source — at each pulse peak (period 45), bloom should aggressively spill blue light onto adjacent panels and even tint the right edge of the comms log
Shield oscilloscope waveform peaks should bloom with cyan glow on each cycle — pulsing energy visualization
LCARS colored bars should have subtle bloom — they are backlit panels, not flat paint
Yellow alert pulsing border should bloom outward, casting yellow light onto adjacent panels

lensFlare.note: JJ Abrams-style lens flare on warp core pulse — horizontal streak with hexagonal elements. This is Trek, lean into it
Shield energy occasionally catches a lens flare — 2-3 instances during the composition

chromaticAberration.note: Present, edges-only. Good. During the hull_damage_flash (frames 200-220), spike to 4px full-frame for 3 frames — the bridge shakes from an impact

filmGrain.note: 0.015 at 24fps. Add ANAMORPHIC grain: stretch grain particles horizontally by 1.5x — Trek is shot anamorphic. This subtly changes the film texture

starfieldParallax.note: Specified as background texture but not detailed. Implement 3 star layers: distant (tiny, slow drift), mid (medium, moderate drift), near (bright, fast drift). Stars should have color temperature variation — blue-white, yellow, red giants. 5 very bright stars should have 4-point diffraction spikes

motionBlur.note: Sweep line needs radial motion blur along its rotation direction — it moves fast

reflectionGlare.note: The tactical display is on a large viewscreen. Add very faint reflections of bridge console lights (orange/blue dots) reflected in the glass

particleEffects.note: Subtle blue streaks moving from right to left across the background — the ship is at warp 6.2, stars should streak very slightly

cameraDrift.note: Bridge camera should have noticeable sway — the ship vibrates at warp, inertial dampers are at 98%, slight motion gets through

rackFocus.note: When yellow alert triggers, focus shifts from tactical to the alert banner — the captain looks up at the status board

dramaticReveals.note: Staggered fade-in center-outward is specified. Enhance: add LCARS boot sequence — panels should draw their borders first (line animation), then fill with data. Classic Trek computer startup aesthetic
When yellow alert triggers, the entire composition should flash yellow for 1 frame (0.1 opacity) then settle into the pulsing border — the klaxon fires

breathingAnimations.note: Engineering subsystem bars should fluctuate slightly — power grid is dynamic, not static
Warp field layers should pulse outward in sync with the warp core — the field breathes with the engine

transitionMoments.description: Yellow alert activation — alert banner transitions, klaxon flash, border pulsing begins, ambient lighting shifts warm-yellow, crew status all show AT STATION, weapons begin charging. The bridge comes alive with purpose

Hull impact moment — port nacelle flashes yellow, camera shake (3px for 5 frames), chromatic aberration spikes, shield oscilloscope waveform distorts briefly, damage report entry in comms log. Brief system flicker (all panels dim 20% for 2 frames then recover)

dataDensityGaps.fix: Add weapons range circles — phaser effective range (4000km) as orange dashed ring, torpedo range (8000km) as red dashed ring. Add a bearing-to-target readout for the active lock
Add ETA readout per waypoint, fuel consumption projection, and a 'DETOUR REQUIRED IF...' advisory zone near the neutral zone
Add DUTY SHIFT indicator (Alpha/Beta/Gamma shift), hours into shift, and RELIEF ETA
232px of unused space. Add SHIP'S LOG entries panel — scrolling captain's log entries mapped to project milestone summaries
Add FIRING SOLUTION quality percentage per bank and COOLDOWN timer for recently fired banks

dataDensityGaps: { "background": true, "fix": "Add starfield that slowly shifts parallax as if the ship is turning slightly during the composition — sells the sensation of travel" }

colorGradingNotes.overallLook: LCARS warm-cool balance — orange and purple frame elements create a warm envelope around cool blue-green tactical data. Grade like Discovery S2 bridge scenes: deep navy backgrounds (#060612 is correct), warm amber instrument panels, cool blue data overlays. Shadows should have purple tint (#0C0818)

keyMoments.shift: Background shifts from #060612 to #0C0C08 — warmer. Panel borders gain subtle yellow tint. Grid lines brighten 30%. The whole bridge warms up with alertness
Flash white for 1 frame, then orange emergency lighting for 10 frames — background shifts to #120A06. Shield panel border flickers red for 3 frames

colorGradingNotes.accentUsage: LCARS palette is well-executed with orange/blue/teal/red/yellow/purple. But the purple (#CC77FF) is underused — it should appear in more decorative LCARS frame elements and as a secondary accent in the star chart
colorGradingNotes.backgroundAtmosphere: Add subtle nebula wisps in the starfield background — very low opacity (0.02) purple and blue gas clouds. The ship is traveling through space, not against a flat void

typographyNotes.fonts: Orbitron for headers is perfect Trek-modern. Rajdhani for body text is clean. Share Tech Mono for data readouts bridges the sci-fi and technical aesthetics
typographyNotes.monospaceReadout: Share Tech Mono is great for tactical data. For the LCARS panels, consider using Michroma or Exo 2 — fonts that feel more integrated with the LCARS design language
typographyNotes.displayFont: Alert condition text at 48px Orbitron 700 is appropriately commanding. The stardate at 28px readoutValue is good. Add glow effect (text-shadow: 0 0 12px currentColor) to alert text
typographyNotes.sizeHierarchy: 9px star chart labels will be too small. Bump to 10px. Tactical bearing labels at 11px are acceptable. Status bar segments could use more size differentiation — ship name at 14px vs mission at 12px

continuousAmbience.sound: Deep thrumming warp core pulse — low frequency (40-60Hz) rhythmic throb every 1.5 seconds. THE sound of Trek. Felt in the chest
Soft electronic sweep tone — rising pitch on each rotation cycle
Ship ambient — constant low hum of EPS conduits, life support air circulation, distant machinery

eventSounds.sound: Klaxon — two-tone alert horn (A4-E4) at 1Hz repeat. The boatswain's whistle precedes it
Comm chirp (classic Trek bo-weep) followed by voice message
Rising electronic whine reaching a satisfying 'locked' tone — phaser banks capacitor charge
Deep impact boom + structural groaning + brief sparking (high frequency crackle)

silenceMoments.description: Tense pause before yellow alert — the unknown contact bearing 170 is identified as potentially hostile. The bridge goes quiet. Then the klaxon
